sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After moving LEFT:
1. Player moved from [28,29]-[31,31] to [28,25]-[31,27] (moved left by ~4 columns)
2. The left c frame at [28,16]-[31,19] is still c (didn't convert to G)
3. The b frame and gray bar expanded to the right
Wait, the left c frame didn't convert to G even though the player moved toward it. Let me check the positions:
- Player at [28,25]-[31,27] (cols 25-27)
- Left c frame at [28,16]-[31,19] (cols 16-19)
The player is at cols 25-27, and the c frame is at cols 16-19. The gap is 25-19-1=5 columns. The player moved from cols 29-31 to cols 25-27, which reduced the gap from 29-19-1=9 to 25-19-1=5. So the player did move closer to the c frame.
But the c frame didn't convert to G. Hmm, maybe the conversion only happens when the player is adjacent to the c frame (gap of 0 or 1).
Wait, let me re-examine the earlier successful conversion:
- Player at [28,32]-[31,35] (cols 32-35), c frame at [28,24]-[31,27] (cols 24-27)
- Player moved LEFT to [28,29]-[31,31] (cols 29-31)
- c frame converted to G
In this case, the gap before was 32-27-1=4, and after was 29-27-1=1. So the gap was reduced from 4 to 1.
Now, the gap before was 29-19-1=9, and after was 25-19-1=5. The gap was reduced from 9 to 5, but the c frame didn't convert.
Maybe the conversion only happens when the gap is reduced to 1 or less. Let me try moving LEFT again to reduce the gap further.
Actually, wait. Looking at the ascii:
```
Row 28: wwccccWWWWWNNNbbbbbbbbbbbb
Row 29: wwcbbcWbbWWNNNbggggggggggb
Row 30: wwcbbcWbbWWNNNbggggggggggb
Row 31: wwccccWWWWWNNNbbbbbbbbbbbb
```
The left c frame is at cols 16-19 (cccc), and the player is at cols 25-27 (NNN). There's a gap of cols 20-24 (5 columns) between them. The gap contains W (white) and some b elements.
Let me try moving LEFT again to reduce the gap.

- Left c frame at [28,16]-[31,19], still c. Gap of ~5 columns.
- c frame only converts to G when gap is reduced to 1 or less.
- Need to move LEFT again to close the gap.
